nativeRange.habitat: Marine intertidal and subtidal zones; rocky substrates in photic zone

avoidWith: herbivorous fish that consume macroalgae (e.g., tangs, urchins in overstocked tanks)

imageCandidates.notes: Product image for Purple Palms Gelidium sp. listing
Product image for Jumbo Gelidium variant listing

summary: A reddish-purple display macroalgae forming dense palm-like clumps of strand growth. Sold under two Mosaic SKUs (Purple Palms and Jumbo Gelidium). Gelidium is one of the principal commercial sources of agar globally.
careNotes:
Purple Palms and Jumbo Gelidium are two Mosaic Macros SKUs of Gelidium sp., a genus in the family Gelidiaceae (order Gelidiales, division Rhodophyta). The Jumbo form is notably taller than the standard Purple Palms variety. Both display a reddish-purple coloration with strand-like growth similar to Chondria, forming thick clumps that tend to vary in shape depending on flow, lighting, and other environmental conditions.

Gelidium is the major commercial agarophyte genus worldwide — Morocco, China, Korea, and Portugal are leading harvest producers. In captivity it is kept purely as an ornamental display specimen.

Mosaic Macros is the sole known hobby source for this species. No AlgaeBarn or Top Shelf Aquatics listing was found. Standard reef parameters are generally advised: temperature around 22–26°C, salinity SG 1.023–1.026, pH 8.0–8.3. Avoid burying the base in sand, which can cause the holdfast to melt; attachment to rockwork or rubble is recommended. Full-spectrum lighting at moderate intensity has been found suitable. Growth tends to be slow relative to green macroalgae like Chaetomorpha. Iron and iodine supplementation is sometimes reported to benefit red macros. Compatible with most reef livestock, though heavy herbivores may graze on it.
breedingNotes: In the wild, Gelidium species reproduce via an alternation of generations (gametophyte/tetrasporophyte). In aquarium settings, propagation is typically achieved by simple fragmentation — detaching and reattaching cuttings to new substrate.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Jumbo Gelidium
additional.notes: Second Mosaic SKU consolidated into this entry; taller growth form of same genus.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros — Macroalgae Care Basics
additional.notes: General reef macroalgae parameters: temp 72–78°F (22–26°C), pH 8.1–8.4, nitrate 5–20 ppm, phosphate 0.02–0.1 ppm. Red macro attachment guidance.
